L1.area: Container image handling and runtime configuration enforcement

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the kernel's ext4 filesystem driver can be exploited through the container runtime's setuid-root mode. The runtime does not enforce restrictions on which filesystem types unprivileged users can mount via kernel drivers in elevated privilege mode, allowing users with write access to raw filesystem data to trigger unpatched kernel memory corruption vulnerabilities for denial of service and potentially privilege escalation.

L3.area: Setuid-mode container image loading and filesystem type permission checks
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the kernel's ext4 filesystem driver can be exploited when the container runtime mounts ext4 overlay or container filesystems using kernel drivers in setuid-root mode. The runtime lacks configuration options to restrict which filesystem types (ext4, squashfs, encrypted) can be mounted in setuid mode by unprivileged users, allowing exploitation of unpatched kernel filesystem vulnerabilities for denial of service and potentially privilege escalation.

ground_truth.reason: The setuid-root installation of Apptainer allows all users to mount ext4 and other filesystem types via kernel drivers without restriction, exposing unpatched kernel use-after-free vulnerabilities (such as CVE-2022-1184) to unprivileged users for denial of service and potential privilege escalation.
